title: A first math note
text:
This is a sample post showing the authoring workflow for the blog. You can edit this file in VS Code with the Quarto extension and use the visual editor when that is helpful.
Inline math works with dollar delimiters, as in \(X_1, \ldots, X_n \sim P\). Display math works with double-dollar delimiters:
\[
\bar X_n = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n X_i.
\]
For example, if \(X_i\) are independent with mean \(\mu\), then
\[
\mathbb{E}[\bar X_n] = \mu.
\]

title: A first math note
text:
Theorem-style writing
You can write theorem-like blocks directly in Markdown:
Lemma. Let \(X\) be integrable. Then for any constant \(a\),
\[
\mathbb{E}[X - a] = \mathbb{E}[X] - a.
\]
Proof. This follows by linearity of expectation. \(\square\)
Citations also work through Pandoc and Quarto; for example, see (Robbins 1970).

title: A first math note
text:
Code
import numpy as np

x = np.array([1.0, 2.0, 3.0])
x.mean()

title: About
text:
I am Neil Xu. I work on statistics and machine learning.
This blog is for technical notes, research ideas, and longer-form writing that benefits from math, citations, code, and reproducible examples.
Main site: https://neilzxu.me
